verses.commentary: Imitating God as dearly loved children means mimicking the Father. Identity as beloved produces imitation. Children naturally reflect their parents; believers reflect God.
Walking in love follows Christ's example of self-giving sacrifice. Love is measured by Christ's cross: fragrant offering to God. True love is costly, not sentimental.
Sexual immorality, impurity, and greed must not even be named among saints. Standards are high: not merely avoiding but not mentioning. Holiness means radical separation from sin.
Obscenity, foolish talk, and coarse joking are out of place; thanksgiving is fitting. Speech reflects the heart. Gratitude replaces vulgarity in the believer's mouth.
Immoral, impure, and greedy people have no inheritance in Christ's kingdom. Greed is idolatry. Lifestyle proves or disproves salvation; persistent sin excludes from the kingdom.
Empty words deceive about sin's consequences; God's wrath comes on disobedience. False teaching minimizes judgment. Reality is severe: wrath follows sin.
Partnership with the disobedient must be avoided. Association implies approval. Believers must distance themselves from those practicing sin.
Once darkness, now light in the Lord demands living as light. Identity transformation requires lifestyle transformation. Being light means acting like light.
Light's fruit is goodness, righteousness, and truth. These qualities naturally emerge from life in the light. Character reflects spiritual condition.
Finding out what pleases the Lord directs behavior. Discernment is active pursuit of God's will. Pleasing God requires intentional discovery.
Darkness's fruitless deeds must be avoided and exposed. Participation and exposure are opposites. Light's nature is revealing darkness.
Mentioning what the disobedient do secretly is shameful. Hidden sins are pervasive and abhorrent. Even speaking of them is disgraceful.
Light exposes and transforms everything. Illumination reveals reality and changes it. What light makes visible becomes light.
The call to wake, rise from the dead, and receive Christ's light is urgent. Spiritual awakening is resurrection. Christ shines on those who awaken.
Careful living as wise, not unwise, requires vigilance. Wisdom shapes choices. Foolishness and wisdom are lifestyle options.
Redeeming time is urgent because days are evil. Opportunity must be seized. Time is limited and the age is hostile.
Understanding the Lord's will opposes foolishness. Wisdom is knowing and doing God's will. Ignorance is culpable.
Drunkenness leads to debauchery; Spirit-fullness is the alternative. Both involve control, but by different agents. The Spirit's filling produces righteousness, not excess.
Spirit-filled people sing to one another and the Lord. Music is corporate worship and mutual edification. Hearts make melody, not just voices.
Always giving thanks for everything in Jesus' name to the Father. Gratitude is constant and comprehensive. Christ mediates thanksgiving to God.
Mutual submission flows from reverence for Christ. Submission is reciprocal in the body. Fear of Christ motivates humble service.
Wives submit to husbands as to the Lord. Marital submission mirrors submission to Christ. Authority structure reflects divine order.
Husband is wife's head as Christ is church's head and Savior. Headship is Christological. Christ's relationship to the church models marriage.
As the church submits to Christ, wives submit to husbands in everything. Church-Christ relationship patterns marriage. Submission is comprehensive.
Husbands must love wives as Christ loved the church and gave Himself for her. Love is sacrificial, not domineering. Christ's self-giving defines husbandly love.
Christ's purpose was making the church holy, cleansing by water and word. Sanctification is Christ's goal. Washing refers to baptism and word.
Christ presents the church to Himself radiant, without blemish, holy and blameless. The church's beauty is Christ's accomplishment. Final presentation is glorious perfection.
Husbands should love wives as their own bodies. Self-love extends to wife. Unity means care for her is self-care.
No one hates his own body but feeds and cares for it, as Christ does the church. Self-care is instinctive. Christ's care for the church models spousal love.
We are members of Christ's body. Union with Christ is organic. Believers are not just affiliated but incorporated.
Genesis's one-flesh union is quoted. Marriage unites two into one. Physical union symbolizes comprehensive unity.
This mystery is profound, referring to Christ and the church. Marriage points beyond itself to divine relationship. Human marriage mirrors cosmic reality.
Each husband must love his wife as himself, and the wife must respect her husband. Love and respect are mutual responsibilities. Both honor the other distinctly.
